items.summary: US President Donald Trump described Pope Leo XIV's accession to the papacy as a meaningful moment for America. But now he has said the pontiff is hurting the Catholic Church, and is weak. Here's how their relationship has developed over the past year.

items.title: Why Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration is raising eyebrows

items.title: How the perfect mother myth was unravelled by a family mystery in Italy
items.summary: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

items.title: Today in history, April 19: Good News Week premiered 30 years ago
items.summary: On April 19, 1996, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

items.title: Ready for Hard Quiz success? It's time to temper those expectations
items.summary: Righteo champ, it's time to put down your toast and impress with your trivia prowess. Let's play, HARD!

items.title: Wallaroos beaten by USA in Pacific Four Series after Kansas City storm delay
items.summary: Australia loses 33-12 to the USA Eagles in their Pacific Four Series after storms in Kansas City delay kick-off by almost three hours.

items.summary: Long before they were cast in bronze, a group of Maltese migrants quietly reshaped who would own farmland across north Queensland.

items.title: Social croquet connects communities as clubs record jump in numbers
items.summary: Golf croquet helps isolated elderly people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

items.title: Marie-Louise Eta takes charge of Union Berlin as first female head coach in Europe's big five leagues
items.summary: Amid a storm of misogynistic criticism, Marie-Louise Eta will become the first woman to take charge of a men's football team in one of Europe's top five leagues. But she just wants everyone to focus on the football.

items.title: Man hospitalised after machete attack at popular Melbourne shopping strip
items.summary: Police say a group of men approached and allegedly stabbed an 18-year-old man at the Eaton Mall in Oakleigh.

items.title: How one walk could spark a national conversation on Australia's past
items.summary: Indigenous leader Travis Lovett sets off on a trek to Canberra, calling for action from the federal government to support a national truth-telling process for First Nations people.

items.title: Lone dingo 'Derek' a concern for some as he roams suburbs in Alice Springs
items.summary: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

items.title: Coorong fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals
items.summary: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

items.title: Disarming Hezbollah in Lebanon is unlikely to be as easy as Donald Trump suggests
items.summary: Donald Trump sold a ceasefire in Lebanon as a step towards peace. But that promise hinges on a condition that Lebanon may not be able to deliver.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz closes as US blockade continues: Iranian official — as it happened

items.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains tax changes
items.summary: The possible budget centrepiece is likely to be finalised much closer to the May deadline than usual this year given global uncertainty, but progress has been made in recent weeks.

items.title: Middle East conflict creates volatile airfare market as prices fluctuate
items.summary: Australians are finding domestic flights for as little as $65 on some routes, while others are paying thousands to travel interstate as airlines quietly reshape the market in response to conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Iran war and energy shock make delivering the federal budget unusually hard
items.summary: The budget's usual timing on the second Tuesday of May is an artifice of political convention. This year's edition just happens to be coinciding with the thunderclap of history in flux.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell
items.summary: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open as Lebanon ceasefire begins

items.title: Aung San Suu Kyi to be freed earlier as Myanmar releases ex-president Win Myint from jail
items.summary: Myanmar's ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi has her prison sentence cut as thousands of prisoners are freed. Meanwhile, the UN reveals 2025 marked the deadliest year on record for Rohingya refugees fleeing Myanmar.

items.title: Parents of Aboriginal teen share anguish during sentence hearing of convicted police officer
items.summary: The parents of 16-year-old Jai Kalani Wright, who died after colliding with a police car, share their emotional testimonies in court as the sentencing hearing begins.

items.title: Albanese joins meeting of leaders to talk about reopening the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages
items.summary: The prime minister of Solomon Islands says Pacific leaders have declared an emergency over the looming threat of fuel shortages, while the Australian government contemplates a plan which could see stocks of diesel held by the US military sent to Pacific nations suffering shortages.

items.title: Viva Geelong refinery fire won't be a problem for us, Tasmanian government says
items.summary: Tasmania has 100 days of petrol and 58 days of diesel supply available, the government says — and that the Geelong refinery fire won't be a problem.

items.title: Mental health nurse Adam McGovern jailed for sexually abusing 14-year-old patient
items.summary: A former West Australian mental health nurse is sentenced to 12 years in jail after being convicted for persistent sexual abuse of a teenager and making "upskirting" videos of women and children.

items.title: Government advisory group recommends delaying NDIS 'robo-planning' changes
items.summary: The federal government has been urged by its own NDIS advisory group to delay the rollout of a controversial new way of determining support plans for participants.

items.title: Victorian political donations website goes dark after High Court struck out election law
items.summary: Victoria has been left without laws to govern party donation disclosures — exposing the state's politics to a flood of unchecked donations.

items.title: Makers of As Deep as the Grave defend use of AI-generated Val Kilmer in movie
items.summary: A trailer for a film with an AI-generated character in the likeness of the late Val Kilmer has been slammed by social media users, but the filmmakers are defending their work.

items.title: Keir Starmer looks to Europe as war in Iran further fractures US relationship
items.summary: Keir Starmer was never a fan of Brexit. Now, under the cover of the war in Iran and a fractured US relationship, he's looking to Europe to return Britain to the fold.

items.title: Taxi drivers say fuel crisis another blow to industry
items.summary: Country taxi drivers say the fuel crisis could prove to be the death knell for the industry in regional areas.

items.title: National Indigenous Cricket Championships offers pathways for young players

items.title: By-election called for seat of Stafford in Brisbane's north after Jimmy Sullivan's death
items.summary: Former Member for Stafford Jimmy Sullivan was found dead at his home earlier this month, after police were called to reports of a non-suspicious death.

items.title: Before the timer reaches zero, 100 bombs have dropped
items.summary: The Israel Defense Forces labelled it Operation Eternal Darkness. Inside Lebanon, it's now called Black Wednesday. ABC NEWS Verify has geolocated the explosions across 10 minutes of chaos.

items.title: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger killed in Salzburg after car hit by train
items.summary: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger, who played for Arsenal and a string of Italian clubs, died on Thursday at the age of 48 after the car he was driving was hit by a train at a crossing near Salzburg.

items.title: Refinery blaze will not bump Australia to stage three of national fuel response
items.summary: Anthony Albanese has cut a trip to Malaysia short to visit Viva Energy Refinery in Geelong, which went up in flames late on Wednesday.

items.title: Endangered southern brown bandicoots bounce back at Brown Hill Creek
items.summary: A small colony of endangered brown bandicoots just south of Adelaide continue to breed as conservationists hope to connect them with other populations.

items.title: Kulbanyi bush tea from NT station hits the shelves in Asia
items.summary: The product is being sent from a remote property in the Northern Territory all the way to Japan, Korea and China.

items.title: French Open bans cameras in player areas after Melbourne criticism
items.summary: Organisers of the French Open have promised that their players will enjoy camera-free privacy in a space of their own, following criticism the Australian Open copped from top stars this year.

items.title: WA state guardianship and administration to be investigated in parliamentary inquiry
items.summary: The state agencies which control the lives of some of Western Australia's most vulnerable people are set to be scrutinised in a parliamentary inquiry, with public submissions now open.

items.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins without Israeli withdrawal
items.summary: Israel and Lebanon agree to a 10-day ceasefire, but Israel's military will remain on Lebanese territory, meaning Hezbollah's conditions have not been met.

items.title: Myanmar's coup leader seeks legitimacy as family wealth surfaces in Thailand
items.summary: As the Myanmar military carried out air strikes during a brutal civil war, the junta chief's family quietly purchased luxury real estate in Thailand.

items.title: AFL-backed helmet company GameGear erases concussion protection claims
items.summary: An AFL-endorsed helmet company has backtracked on its claims of "superior protection against concussion" and the league is now also facing a complaint lodged with the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission on the matter.

items.title: Consumers are cutting back on spending amid higher risk of global recession
items.summary: As fuel costs surge and supply fears grow, businesses and households say the economic shock from the war in the Middle East is already hitting home.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown
items.summary: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

items.title: Donald Trump announces 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon
items.summary: The US president says he "had excellent conversations" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

items.title: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz open, thousands of Lebanese return home — as it happened
items.summary: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz, as thousands of displaced people in Lebanon are returning to their homes following a 10-day ceasefire deal with Israel.

items.title: How to tell if your dog is in pain
items.summary: New research suggests that many dog owners are not skilled in recognising pain in their pets.

items.title: Administrators tell Liberty Bell Bay workers to take leave without pay or be sacked
items.summary: About 175 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter, Liberty Bell Bay, have been told they will be laid off at the end of next week unless they take leave without pay.

items.title: Farmers 'running on empty' welcome arrival of diesel to the Port of Newcastle
items.summary: A massive crude oil tanker laden with diesel has docked in the Port of Newcastle to replenish regional New South Wales supplies, as the Middle East unrest continues.

items.title: NRL six-again rule should be scrapped, according to coach Ricky Stuart
items.summary: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says the NRL has lost the fabric of its game, leading to anger over the league's controversial six-again rule change.

items.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in seat of Narungga won by One Nation
items.summary: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

items.title: Australia's rarest reptile, the kungaka skink, found in far west NSW national park
items.summary: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.

items.title: Some country towns could lose more than half of their shade due to aging trees
items.summary: Some country towns could lose up to 80 per cent of their shade due to aging trees, but replacing them is not simple for councils.

items.title: Investigation launched into Papua New Guinea Defence Force recruitment processes following soldier protest
items.summary: A small group of officers deserted their posts this week in protest over alleged government corruption.

items.title: Fuel-saving campaign prompts open letter to prime minister from regional Queensland council
items.summary: A Queensland council sends an open letter to the Albanese government calling for a national fuel tracker to protect rural economies, while criticising the federal fuel-saving campaign.

items.title: US jury finds Ticketmaster and Live Nation have anticompetitive monopoly
items.summary: A New York jury finds concert giant Live Nation and its Ticketmaster subsidiary have a harmful monopoly over big concert venues.

items.title: From a new Australian musical to buzzy horror, here's what's in cinemas in April
items.summary: Plus a powerful look Palestinian life through multiple generations and a buzzy Japanese video game adaptation makes it to Australian shores.

items.title: Josh Hazlewood helps Royal Challengers Bengaluru to IPL win over Lucknow
items.summary: Josh Hazlewood has produced another masterful display in the IPL to rocket Royal Challengers Bengaluru back to the top of the league.
